task_completion.description: Open and read the project documentation containing unit rates
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, document viewer, or terminal displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/cost_rates_spec.txt

task_completion.description: Access the pre-loaded FZK-Haus model in Bonsai/BlenderBIM
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender application open with the Bonsai/BlenderBIM add-on active and the FZK-Haus 3D model visible in the viewport

task_completion.description: Create a formal cost schedule named 'Pre-Tender Estimate'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Costing or Cost Schedule panel in Bonsai/BlenderBIM showing a newly created schedule with the name 'Pre-Tender Estimate'

task_completion.description: Populate the schedule with cost items for major element types
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cost schedule interface displaying individual line items for 'External Walls', 'Internal Walls', 'Doors', and 'Windows'

task_completion.description: Apply the specified unit rates to the cost items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cost schedule showing the correct unit rates applied to the items (External Walls: 285.0, Internal Walls: 125.0, Doors: 750.0, Windows: 650.0)

task_completion.description: Save the IFC project with the embedded cost schedule
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender's 'Save Project' or 'Export IFC' dialog showing the path /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_cost_schedule.ifc, or a file manager/terminal verifying the file's creation at this path

integrity.description: The agent used the Bonsai/BlenderBIM graphical interface to create the schedule
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's Costing/BIM panels rather than just running a Python script to bypass the UI

integrity.description: The agent genuinely saved an IFC file from the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The output file was generated via Blender's save/export functionality, not by creating a dummy file using terminal commands like 'touch'

integrity.description: The agent retrieved the rates from the actual document rather than guessing
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen opening the cost_rates_spec.txt file before entering the values into BlenderBIM

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification document is located at /home/ga/Desktop/cost_rates_spec.txt. The expected cost schedule name is exactly 'Pre-Tender Estimate'. The required cost items to be added are 'External Walls', 'Internal Walls', 'Doors', and 'Windows'. The unit rates must be exactly: External Walls: 285.0, Internal Walls: 125.0, Doors: 750.0, Windows: 650.0. The final output file must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_cost_schedule.ifc.
